finding_results.notes: Differentiated institutional voices across the three flagged exchanges: Renee now reads as situationally colloquial and pressure-responsive rather than policy-scripted, Brielle retains rigid call-center compliance cadence, and the wire clerk carries a brisk contemporary service-register with distinct informal phrasing. Eli's pressured replies were tightened with higher contraction use and sharper leverage-driven turns.

summary: Revised dialogue in the impound, lender-call, and wire-counter exchanges to enforce clearer idiolect separation, stronger pressure cadence, and less swappable institutional speech while preserving chapter continuity and established character register.
